channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Rubio could face an unfriendly reception from close G7 allies over Trump's policies
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio may be walking into unusually unfriendly territory this week when he meets his counterparts from the Group of 7 industrialized democracies.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UN launches reform initiative as it nears its 80th anniversary and faces funding cuts
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.N. chief launched a new initiative Wednesday to reform the United Nations as it approaches its 80th anniversary, saying the 193-member global organization needs an urgent update to deal with major funding reductions and still tackle the world's challenges.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump envoys begin Russia outreach after Ukraine breakthrough
item.description: Trump administration officials are reaching out to their Russian counterparts as the White House says the ball is in Moscow’s court to agree to a ceasefire in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump says Ireland cheats the US as its leader joins him to celebrate St. Patrick's Day
item.description: President Donald Trump is hosting Ireland's prime minister, Micheál Martin, for the annual St. Patrick's Day celebration at the White House.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US and Russian spy chiefs speak amid Ukraine war talks
item.description: CIA Director John Ratcliffe and Sergei Naryshkin, chief of the Russian Foreign Intelligence Service, or SVR, spoke over the phone Tuesday amid recent peace talks with Ukraine, endorsing a 30-day ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Trump says tariffs will be 'positive'; Vance pushes Russia for ceasefire
item.description: “The United States of America is going to take back a lot of what was stolen from it by other countries.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Pressed to accept a ceasefire in Ukraine, Putin seems likely to seek his own conditions
item.description: By signaling its openness to a ceasefire, Ukraine has handed the Kremlin a difficult challenge at a time when the Russian military has the upper hand in the war: Should Moscow accept a truce and abandon hopes of making new gains, or should it reject the offer and risk derailing a cautious rapprochem
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Reagan 'would be heartbroken' over Trump isolating US: Patti Davis
item.description: Patti Davis, former President Reagan's daughter, believes that her father would be heartbroken by America's shift towards isolationism under President Trump, as it goes against the values of the country he instilled in her.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Conservative commentator asks Irish PM why he 'let' Rosie O'Donnell move to his country
item.description: A conservative commentator on Wednesday asked Irish Prime Minister Micheál Martin why he allowed American comedian Rosie O'Donnell to move to the country.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: US toughens visitor registration rules for Canadians
item.description: The United States is cracking down on long-term visits from Canadians by now requiring visitors in the U.S. longer than 30 days to register with the government.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Greenland opposition party wins election held in Trump's shadow
item.description: Greenland voters have expressed strong support for sovereignty, with the Demokraatit party winning nearly 30% of the vote, while President Trump continues to push for an annexation of the island with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill

item.title: Greenland's center-right party pulls off upset victory as Trump seeks control
item.description: Greenland's center-right Demokraatit party saw a sharp increase in support as it won the country's election on Tuesday. Independence became a central issue as President Donald Trump talked of a U.S. takeover.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Austria's new government is stopping family reunions immediately for migrants
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — The new Austrian government said Wednesday that family reunion procedures for migrants will be immediately halted because the country is no longer able to absorb newcomers adequately.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and PHILIPP JENNE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistan says insurgents who attacked a passenger train killed 21 hostages while all others freed
item.description: Pakistani officials say insurgents who attacked a passenger train carrying 440 people in the southwest killed 21 hostages but security forces rescued the others.
item.dc:creator: Abdul Sattar And Munir Ahmed, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pakistani insurgents attack a train carrying hundreds of people and take hostages
item.description: QUETTA, Pakistan (AP) — Pakistani insurgents attacked a passenger train carrying several hundred people as it passed through a tunnel on Tuesday and claimed to have taken more than 100 hostages, though officials later said at least 104 were rescued.
item.dc:creator: ABDUL SATTAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel kills 8 Palestinians in Gaza as Hamas says ceasefire talks resume
item.description: Israeli fire has killed eight Palestinians in the Gaza Strip and three more in the occupied West Bank over the past 24 hours, Palestinian officials said Tuesday, even as a fragile ceasefire with Hamas in Gaza has largely held since late January.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US resumes military aid and intelligence sharing as Ukraine says it is open to a 30-day ceasefire
item.description: JEDDAH, Saudi Arabia (AP) — The Trump administration lifted its suspension of military aid and intelligence sharing for Ukraine, and Kyiv signaled that it was open to a 30-day ceasefire in the war with Russia, pending Moscow’s agreement, American and Ukrainian officials said Tuesday following talks
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clowns and musicians bring the joy of Carnival to sick children in a Rio hospital
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Clowns complete with red noses and tutus delivered the joy of Carnival to sick children in a Rio de Janeiro hospital on Tuesday, bringing the ebullient festivities normally found on the streets straight to the young patients and their caregivers.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES and DIARLEI RODRIGUES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada suspends 25% electricity tariffs on US
item.description: This comes after what Ford said was a "productive" conversation with U.S. Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: British tourist detained in ICE center after visa suspension: Family
item.description: In a statement to NewsNation, an ICE spokesperson said Burke was detained by the agency "for violation of the terms and conditions of her admission.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: US, Ukraine talk peace as Kyiv, Moscow trade drone attacks
item.description: Officials from Ukraine and the United States have gathered in Saudi Arabia to discuss a potential limited ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: US-flagged tanker collides with container ship near UK
item.description: Thirty-two people were brought ashore, conditions unknown, after a vessel collided with a tanker carrying Jet-A1 fuel causing multiple explosions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ex-Philippine President Duterte shrugs off possible arrest by ICC for drug war during trip to Hong Kong
item.description: Ex-Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte visited Hong Kong on Sunday, leading to speculation that he may be attempting to evade a possible arrest warrant by the ICC.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Romania blocks frontrunner from postponed presidential race
item.description: Protests have erupted in Romania after authorities barred Calin Georgescu, a candidate critical of NATO, from running in May's presidential election re-run.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada braces for new leader: Here are Trudeau’s potential successors
item.description: Canada is bracing for Prime Minister Justin Trudeau’s successor, who will follow the Liberal Party leader that has led U.S.’s neighbor to the north since the Obama administration, The Associated Press reported. The change in leadership will come amid frosty U.S.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Scotland golf resort vandalized by pro-Palestinian activists
item.description: A pro-Palestinian activist group has claimed responsibility for the crimes, which included defacing a building and digging up greens.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Aid efforts in Gaza slow as millions of promised USAID dollars do not arrive, agency officials say
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Trump administration’s cuts to USAID have frozen hundreds of millions of dollars in contractual payments to aid groups, leaving them paying out of pocket to preserve a fragile ceasefire, according to officials from the U.S. humanitarian agency.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mother of Abbey Gate victim says Biden ‘robbed’ family of justice
item.description: "If we can take one terrorist off the list of harming Americans, especially our soldiers, then that is a good day," Paula Knauss Selph told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas disregards Trump’s ‘last warning’ over hostage release
item.description: Hamas' response is an apparent dismissal of President Donald Trump's "last warning" issued on social media.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump issues 'last warning' to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday issued what he called a “last warning” to Hamas to release all remaining hostages held in Gaza, directing a sharply worded message after the White House confirmed that he had recently dispatched an envoy for unprecedented direct talks with the mi
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
